subject: Cloud Computing
title: The Well-Architected Framework
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Well-Architected Framework.

hook.text: AWS published a checklist for building cloud systems right. It became the industry's architecture bible.

buildup.text: The framework covers six pillars: security, reliability, performance, cost optimization, operations, and sustainability.

discovery.text: Azure and Google published their own versions. The pillars are similar because good architecture is universal.

twist.text: No system excels at all six pillars simultaneously. Every architecture makes trade-offs between them.

climax.text: The framework isn't a recipe — it's a conversation starter. It forces teams to think about trade-offs explicitly.

punchline.text: Good architecture is about trade-offs. The framework makes them visible.

quiz.question: What is the purpose of the Well-Architected Framework?
options: To guide teams in making explicit architecture trade-offs across key pillars
To provide a single correct way to build all cloud systems
To certify that a cloud system will never experience failures
